text.title: Frontmatter authoring rules
text.summary: Frontmatter must be short, trigger-rich, and concrete — the description IS the routing prompt.

rule.statement: Every SKILL.md frontmatter must declare name, description, and (when relevant) tier and user-invocable; missing description means the skill is invisible to the router.
rule.rationale: Claude's skill router only sees what the frontmatter exposes — fields it doesn't list cannot influence activation.

list.item: name — short, hyphenated, unique within the surface (e.g. faion-deploy).
description — first sentence "Use when ...", remaining sentences enumerate triggers and steps.
tier — free | solo | pro | geek (faion-network internal partition).
user-invocable — true if the user can call it directly via /name; false for auto-discovered skills.

section.title: Trigger-rich descriptions
section.p: The description is matched against the user's message; load it with verbs and nouns the user actually says.

rule.statement: Description must front-load action verbs (deploy, lint, generate, post, schedule) and concrete nouns (faion.net, neromedia, telegram, gatsby) instead of abstract category terms.
rule.rationale: Routers do soft string matching — "publish a new article to neromedia" reliably activates a description containing "neromedia" and "publish article", but not one that says "content workflows".

example.code: description: Use when user asks to publish, schedule, or queue a post to a NERO TG channel (neromedia, pashtelka, longlife, ender) or to inspect a posting pipeline. Wraps tg-send, post queue, classification.
example.note: Lists every channel name, every operation verb — wide surface for the router.

section.title: Length cap

rule.statement: Frontmatter must stay under 30 non-blank lines; if the description exceeds this, split into multiple skills with narrower triggers.
rule.rationale: Frontmatter is in the system prompt for every request — every line costs across the entire session.

antipattern.description: Documenting the procedure in the description field instead of in SKILL.md body.
antipattern.reason: The procedure now loads on every request — defeats the entire point of leveling.

section.reference: Skills best practices — frontmatter and trigger phrasing.
